mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding capacity and economic momentum, which supports corporate earnings growth and is consistently followed by positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If over a 3-year rolling window, the 20-day forward return following months with INDPRO above prior month is negative more than 50% of the time, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labour market slack depresses consumer spending and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If within any 2-year period, more than 40% of triggered instances produce positive 20-day forward returns, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate combined with rising consumer price index signals that monetary tightening is underway to combat inflation, which compresses equity valuations and reliably produces negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If over 3 years, the mean 20-day return following joint positive growth in both series is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery regime, rising retail sales (RSAFS) confirm consumer demand momentum, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If during NBER-dated expansion phases, the 60-day forward return is negatively correlated with RSAFS growth (rank correlation < 0) over any 5-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and consumer wealth, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If more than 30% of weekly triggers produce positive 5-day forward returns, or if the average 5-day return is positive, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labour market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, consistently leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return following months with sequential PAYEMS increases is not positive on average (mean <= 0) over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When year-on-year CPI growth exceeds 2.5%, inflationary pressure builds that forces eventual monetary tightening, which lowers equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If triggered months are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the median return is positive, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market where the unemployment rate is below 4%, further increases in payroll employment intensify wage pressures and reduce profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If within periods where UNRATE < 4%, the 20-day forward return following a positive PAYEMS month is positive on average (mean > 0), the hypothesis is rejected.
